polymarket_events_sample.title: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit__ by end of March?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket leans heavily against Crude Oil (CL) surpassing the key threshold by March 31, with yes shares trading around 25% implied probability, reflecting bearish pressures from surging U.S. shale output—now at record 13.2 million bpd per EIA data—and softening Chinese demand amid weak factory activity. WTI futures hover near $78/bbl, down 5% monthly, as ample global inventories offset Red Sea disruptions and voluntary OPEC+ cuts of 2.2 million bpd. Key upside risks include tighter sanctions on Russian exports or hotter-than-expected inflation delaying Fed cuts; watch Wednesday's EIA storage report for a potential 2-3 million barrel draw. Historical EOM rallies average 2-4% absent major catalysts, underscoring downside bias.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 24% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, reflecting his proactive national profile as California governor, including early visits to Iowa and New Hampshire, strong fundraising, and vocal opposition to Republican policies. The wide-open field stems from Kamala Harris's diminished standing post-2024, creating opportunities for figures like Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (8%), whose progressive appeal and youth energize the base, and Jon Ossoff (6%), bolstered by his Georgia Senate victory in a battleground state. Key differentiators include executive experience for governors like Newsom, legislative records for senators, and media savvy for rising stars. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results, major endorsements, or responses to economic or foreign policy challenges.

polymarket_events_sample.title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?

This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating Israel-Iran military exchanges, capped by Israel's October 26 strikes on Iranian missile facilities, have sharply reduced near-term odds of a US-Iran ceasefire, with trader consensus reflecting minimal diplomatic momentum. Iran minimized the damage while signaling potential retaliation, amid persistent proxy clashes via Hezbollah in Lebanon and Houthis in Yemen, where US backing for Israel sustains tensions. Absent official negotiation channels or nuclear deal progress, the November 5 US presidential election emerges as the key catalyst, as the outcome could recalibrate American pressure on Tehran and influence de-escalation paths.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
polymarket_events_sample.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's 2028 Presidential Election Winner market places JD Vance at 19.6%, buoyed by his vice-presidential role and alignment with Trump-era GOP priorities, narrowly ahead of Gavin Newsom's 16.8% as a prominent Democratic counterweight and Marco Rubio's 12.0% as a Senate heavyweight offering broader appeal. The clustered top odds reflect an open-field dynamic post-2024, with distant primaries, undefined party nominations, and variables like Trump's endorsement influence or Democratic rebuilding keeping probabilities fragmented. Potential separators include 2026 midterm results signaling momentum, early fundraising tallies, national polling surges, or cabinet appointments that elevate profiles amid economic or foreign policy shifts.

polymarket_events_sample.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's Euro 2024 victory over England vaults them to the top of trader consensus at 15.4%, bolstered by young stars like Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams, though Rodri's ACL tear clouds their midfield stability into 2025 qualifiers. England's 12.8% follows their final appearance and Thomas Tuchel's arrival as coach, while France (10.5%) and Copa America champion Argentina (10.3%) leverage Mbappé's brilliance and Messi's experience amid Scaloni's continuity. Brazil lags at 8.6% despite talent like Vinícius Júnior, hampered by inconsistent form. This bunched race reflects parity across Europe's depth and South America's volatility, with UEFA/CONMEBOL qualifiers, aging icons, and host auto-bids for USA/Canada/Mexico injecting uncertainty over the two-year horizon.
